content: Empiric therapy for suspected BSI/sepsis: vancomycin (MRSA coverage) + antipseudomonal beta-lactam (cefepime, piperacillin-tazobactam, meropenem) based on local antibiogram and patient risk factors. Add echinocandin if Candida risk (TPN, abdominal surgery, prolonged antibiotics, femoral catheter). De-escalate based on culture results. CRBSI management: remove catheter if S. aureus, Candida, Pseudomonas, Bacillus, Mycobacterium, or if severe sepsis/hemodynamic instability; consider antibiotic lock therapy with catheter salvage for coagulase-negative staphylococci if no complications. Duration: uncomplicated Gram-negative 7 days, S. aureus minimum 14 days from first negative culture (longer if metastatic foci), Candida 14 days after clearance. Echocardiography recommended for S. aureus bacteremia to rule out endocarditis.
